pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards Video - Medical alerts from a microwearable sticker

pageCaption.value: Biomedical engineer and 2012 Rolex Laureate Mark Kendall is on a mission to revolutionize modern medicine.

WaPageTitle.value: Medical alerts from a sticker on your hand - Video

metadescription.value: Scientist and 2012 Rolex Laureate Mark Kendall has produced microwearable sensors that provide critical medical information. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Medical alerts from a sticker on your hand - Video

text.value: Biomedical engineer and 2012 Rolex Laureate Mark Kendall is on a mission to revolutionize modern medicine. His Nanopatch, a pain-free vaccine requiring no refrigeration, makes it possible for anyone to deliver immunization anywhere. The patch proved so effective that it is undergoing clinical trials for several diseases including pandemic influenza, measles and polio. Currently, the Australian scientist is developing microwearable sensors worn on the surface of the skin that provide critical information to make timely treatment decisions.

pageTitle.value: Grégoire Courtine's Life in motion - Perpetual Planet - Video

pageCaption.value: Neuroscientist Grégoire Courtine's revolutionary "digital bridge" technology could change the lives of those with spinal cord injuries. Learn more on rolex.org.

pageCaption.value: Piyush Tewari is determined to save thousands of lives by ensuring that most accident victims in Delhi receive rapid medical care. His foundation, in partnership with hospitals and a medical science institute, has already provided training sessions in basic life support to over 2,000 police officers, as well as 500 ordinary citizens.

pageCaption.value: Billy Lee Lasley’s invention of a non-invasive method for determining the sex of birds — designed to help protect endangered species such as eagles — has slowly been adopted by researchers around the world. Perhaps more importantly, his work has proved to have other uses, including monitoring the reproductive health of women exposed to chemicals in the environment.

pageCaption.value: Professor Mark Kendall is working on the development of the needle-free ‘Nanopatch’, to revolutionize delivery of vaccines in the developing world making them cheaper and potentially saving the lives of the millions of people who die from infectious diseases.

pageCaption.value: Superbugs, resistant to antibiotics, are a threat to humanity. Saudi Arabian microbiologist Hosam Zowawi is combating the misuse of antibiotics by developing groundbreaking diagnostic tests and an awareness campaign in the Gulf states.
